query_strategy_description: 20 web-search queries grouped into 6 buckets that map to the regulatory_brief's 14 sections. Use WebSearch (native Claude tool). Prefer national gazettes, ministry / agency websites, parliamentary records, official trade-body comment letters, and reputable trade press. Avoid named-firm aggregators (Mordor/Frost/etc.). Source-mix target is heavier secondary (70%) than market_analysis because policy briefs cite government documents verbatim.

query_buckets.purpose: Locate the actual statute / decree / circular text and codify the current framework → drives 07_framework_regulators + 08_policy_timeline
query_buckets.expected_tag_mix: secondary 90% / primary 10% (KIRA synthesis when statute interpretation requires triangulation)

query_buckets.purpose: Find actual enforcement actions, fines, license suspensions, court rulings → drives 09_recent_changes_detail + the winner/loser side of 11_compliance_winners_losers
expected_sources: national business press (e.g. The Jakarta Post, VnExpress, Bangkok Post, BusinessWorld)
trade press specialising in the affected industry
query_buckets.expected_tag_mix: secondary 80% / estimate 20% (enforcement-action counts often need KIRA aggregation from multi-source)

query_buckets.purpose: Surface industry-association comment letters, position papers, public submissions → drives 10_pending_lobby
expected_sources: industry trade association websites (KADIN, JCCI, AmCham, EuroCham, FTA-of-Thai-Industries, VCCI, etc.)
policy think tanks (CSIS Jakarta, ERIA, ISEAS Yusof Ishak)
query_buckets.note: Coalition names use official short-form. NEVER cite individual firm-level lobbying — keep at association level (anti-positioning rule extends here).

query_buckets.purpose: ASEAN peer policy approaches on the same domain → drives 12_cross_border_comparison
expected_sources: regional law-firm comparison memos (read but cite only if methodology is disclosed)
query_buckets.note: Peer selection prioritises ASEAN-6 economies (ID/VN/TH/PH/MY/SG). Avoid lumping CN/JP/KR — those are reference economies, not peers.

query_buckets.purpose: Compliance cost benchmarks, operator-level burden estimates → drives 11_compliance_winners_losers
queries: {{policy_topic}} compliance burden per-firm cost benchmark ASEAN
expected_sources: Big-4 advisory tax/legal practice publications (read but never cite by name — use 'industry advisors estimate' instead)
query_buckets.expected_tag_mix: secondary 50% / estimate 50% (RIAs are rare in SEA outside Singapore/Malaysia; most compliance-cost figures need KIRA triangulation)
query_buckets.note: Triangulation method: anchor on a published number from one source × scale by operator count or industry size for {{country}}. Document method in chart source line: e.g. 'SOURCE: KIRA ESTIMATE — RIA ANCHOR × OPERATOR COUNT. ANCHOR: USD 12K/OPERATOR YR-1 [SECONDARY]'.

query_buckets.purpose: News tracking on pending or proposed changes → drives 10_pending_lobby + the 'pending' marker on 08_policy_timeline
expected_sources: parliamentary / DPR / National Assembly committee records
specialist policy newsletters (e.g. Tilleke & Gibbins / Baker McKenzie alerts — read but cite as 'specialist legal counsel' if used)
query_buckets.note: Pending changes must be flagged with a status descriptor: 'draft' (not yet introduced), 'proposed' (introduced, no vote), 'in-committee' (under review), 'pending royal assent' (Thailand/Malaysia), 'pending issuance' (decree signed but not yet gazetted).

query_execution_rules.parallelism: Run queries in parallel within a bucket (up to 5 concurrent). Sequential across buckets so later buckets can reference earlier findings (e.g. the framework bucket identifies regulators that the enforcement bucket then queries by name).
result_filtering: Discard results from named-firm aggregators (Mordor, Frost, Euromonitor, IMARC, Synovate, Ipsos) — anti-positioning rule
PREFER results from the issuing body's own website over second-hand reporting — for statute text, gazette > trade press > Wikipedia
If a statute is referenced by multiple sources with conflicting effective dates, treat the gazette / official journal date as canonical and downgrade conflicting sources
For enforcement actions, prefer regulator press release > court ruling text > trade-press write-up
Lobby positions: only cite if attributable to a named association or coalition; rumour-mill reporting on 'industry sources' should NOT be cited
query_execution_rules.estimate_methodology_template: Anchored estimate: {{anchor_metric}} × {{ratio}} = {{output}}. E.g. 'RIA from Malaysia USD 12K/operator yr-1 × {{country}} operator count 4,200 → annualised compliance burden USD 50m [estimate]'. Always disclose method in the chart source line or inline.
query_execution_rules.research_data_output_format: Aggregate findings into a single JSON file matching the shape of references/research_data_R0152.json — top-level keys for each major data bucket. For regulatory_brief, additional top-level keys: `statute_table` (list of {{statute_id, name, year, status, scope}}), `timeline_markers` (list of {{date, label, body, winner_loser, source}}), `regulator_map` (list of {{ministry, enforcement_arm, scope}}), `lobby_positions` (list of {{coalition, position, source_letter_url}}).

template_variable_resolution.{{policy_topic}}: From topic parser, normalized (e.g. 'EV subsidy regime', 'halal certification', 'data localization', 'FX controls')
template_variable_resolution.{{industry}}: From topic parser — the industry the policy regulates (e.g. 'EVs', 'food & beverage', 'fintech', 'financial services'). May overlap with policy_topic for industry-specific regimes.
template_variable_resolution.{{ministry_likely}}: Pre-mapped lookup table based on country × policy domain. Examples — VN+EV: 'Ministry of Industry and Trade (MOIT)' + 'Ministry of Finance (MOF)'; ID+halal: 'BPJPH' + 'Ministry of Religious Affairs (Kemenag)'; TH+FX: 'Bank of Thailand (BOT)' + 'Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)'; PH+data: 'National Privacy Commission (NPC)' + 'Department of Information and Communications Technology (DICT)'.
template_variable_resolution.{{regulator_name}}: Filled iteratively as 01_statute_regulation_text bucket runs — names extracted from statute text
template_variable_resolution.{{peer_country_1}}: First ASEAN peer country for comparison — selected based on economic comparability + topic relevance. Default selection logic: if {{country}} = VN/ID/PH → peer = TH; if {{country}} = TH → peer = MY; if {{country}} = MY/SG → peer = TH or ID. Override-able by topic parser if specific peers are economically relevant.
template_variable_resolution.{{year}}: Topic year (parser output, default current year)
